Risk Statement: If privileged access is not reviewed on a recurring basis, excessive access may persist and increase the likelihood of unauthorized system changes or data access.
If high-risk vendors are onboarded without timely security assessment, the organization may inherit unmanaged vendor security exposure.
If audit findings are not remediated within defined timelines, control deficiencies may persist and weaken audit readiness.
If approved policy exceptions are not periodically reviewed, temporary risk acceptances may become permanent unmanaged exposure.
If incident response evidence is not centrally retained, the organization may lack defensible records for investigations, audits, and regulatory inquiries.
If remediation tasks are closed without independent validation, unresolved control gaps may remain hidden from governance reporting.
If leadership does not receive consistent risk reporting, enterprise risk decisions may be delayed or based on incomplete information.
If risks are not mapped to business and technology assets, governance reporting may lack traceability between exposure and business impact.
If vendor remediation activities are not tracked through governance workflows, unresolved vendor findings may remain unmanaged.
If risks, controls, policies, and issues are not consistently mapped, audit traceability and governance reporting may be incomplete.
